content.problemStatement:
In a cricket game, an over is a set of six valid deliveries of balls performed by one player ― the bowler for this over.

Consider a cricket game with a series of NNN overs (numbered 111 through NNN) played by KKK players (numbered 111 through KKK). Each player may be the bowler for at most LLL overs in total, but the same player may not be the bowler for any two consecutive overs. Assign exactly one bowler to each over in such a way that these rules are satisfied or determine that no such assignment exists.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, KKK and LLL.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If there is no valid assignment of bowlers to overs, print a single line containing the integer −1-1−1.
Otherwise, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should be the number of the player assigned as the bowler for the iii-th over.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The following is a valid assignment:

Bowler 1 bowls the 111-st over.
Bowler 2 bowls the 222-nd and 444-th overs.
Bowler 3 bowls the 333-rd over.

It is valid since no bowler bowls more than 222 overs and each two consecutive overs have different bowlers.
Example case 2: There is no valid assignment in which each of 444 players bowls at most 111 over out of 555.
